title: Helictotrichon newtonii
intro: Helictotrichon newtonii, also known as Newton's silver grass, is a species of flowering plant in the grass family Poaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects, which may help to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Helictotrichon newtonii include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Helictotrichon newtonii include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Helictotrichon newtonii is classified as a species within the genus Helictotrichon, which is a group of grasses in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the order Poales, which includes various families of grasses and their allies. The class is Liliopsida, which comprises monocots that have a single cotyledon in their seeds. The phylum is Magnoliophyta, a group of flowering plants with seed-producing structures. The kingdom it belongs to is Plantae, which includes all plant species.
